verses.text: Ahaziah becomes king and walks in the ways of Ahab's house, for his mother Athaliah is his counsellor to do wickedly. A godless mother shapes a godless son. The closest counsel, when it is corrupt, can ruin a young heart and a whole reign.

Ahaziah becomes king and walks in the ways of Ahab's house, for his mother Athaliah is his counsellor to do wickedly. A godless mother shapes a godless son. The closest counsel, when it is corrupt, can ruin a young heart and a whole reign.

Ahaziah becomes king and walks in the ways of Ahab's house, for his mother Athaliah is his counsellor to do wickedly. A godless mother shapes a godless son. The closest counsel, when it is corrupt, can ruin a young heart and a whole reign.

Ahaziah becomes king and walks in the ways of Ahab's house, for his mother Athaliah is his counsellor to do wickedly. A godless mother shapes a godless son. The closest counsel, when it is corrupt, can ruin a young heart and a whole reign.

Following Ahab's counsel, Ahaziah joins Israel's war at Ramoth-gilead and goes to visit wounded Joram. Bad company leads him step by step into danger. The friendships we choose quietly chart the road our lives will take.

Following Ahab's counsel, Ahaziah joins Israel's war at Ramoth-gilead and goes to visit wounded Joram. Bad company leads him step by step into danger. The friendships we choose quietly chart the road our lives will take.

His downfall, the text says, 'was of God': caught up in Jehu's purge, he is found and slain, buried only for godly Jehoshaphat's sake. Providence overrules even a king's wandering. God's hand is in the fall of the wicked, yet He honours past faith in a forebear.

His downfall, the text says, 'was of God': caught up in Jehu's purge, he is found and slain, buried only for godly Jehoshaphat's sake. Providence overrules even a king's wandering. God's hand is in the fall of the wicked, yet He honours past faith in a forebear.

His downfall, the text says, 'was of God': caught up in Jehu's purge, he is found and slain, buried only for godly Jehoshaphat's sake. Providence overrules even a king's wandering. God's hand is in the fall of the wicked, yet He honours past faith in a forebear.

Athaliah destroys all the royal seed, but Jehoshabeath hides the infant Joash in the temple six years while the usurper reigns. One hidden child preserves the line of David. When the promise seems all but snuffed out, God keeps a flame alive in secret.
